title: Dendrophthoe chrysantha
intro: Dendrophthoe chrysantha, commonly known as golden mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species that attaches itself to the branches of its host tree, obtaining nutrients through a network of threads called haustoria. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its purported health benefits of reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Bioactive constituents of Dendrophthoe chrysantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Dendrophthoe chrysantha is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used to make infusions, capsules, and injections for treating a range of health conditions.
